meta.note: internal_links_to lists blog IDs. Every blog always links to #1 (master pillar) and #88 (FAQ/AEO pillar). Every blog links to its category pillar. Additional links are contextually relevant only.

cta_templates.A: If this raised a question about your own situation — browse the blog for more, or WhatsApp directly for a quick answer: [WhatsApp Number].
cta_templates.B: If your land acquisition payout has arrived and you are deciding what to do with it — one conversation gives you a clear picture. WhatsApp: [WhatsApp Number]. No obligation.
cta_templates.C: If you are looking at Mohali property from abroad and want an honest read before committing — I do video consultations. WhatsApp or book a call: [Booking Link].
cta_templates.D: If you are evaluating a specific project and want an independent read before committing — 15 minutes, no pitch. WhatsApp: [WhatsApp Number].
cta_templates.E: If what you read describes your situation — one 15-minute call. I will tell you directly what I would do in your position. Book: [Booking Link] or WhatsApp: [WhatsApp Number].

icp_profiles.HNI: Self-made Tricity-based business owner, age 38–58, pharma/manufacturing/trade, ₹3–8Cr ticket, wants ROI logic not emotional pitch, fears buying at peak or bad developer, respects people who disagree with him honestly, heavy referrer within business network.
icp_profiles.CXO: Senior corporate executive VP/Director/CXO at MNC or large corp, age 35–52, ₹2–5Cr ticket, data-driven, Googles everything, spouse is co-decision-maker, takes 3–6 months to decide, needs shareable project brief.
icp_profiles.LandSeller: Punjab agricultural/rural land seller post-acquisition, ₹50L–5Cr payout, scared of making wrong decision with life-changing money, family decision including elder generation, 24-month capital gains window, often tempted by FD as safe option.
icp_profiles.NRI: Punjabi diaspora in UK/Canada/Australia/UAE, ₹2–15Cr ticket, biggest fear is being cheated because he is far away, often burned before, watches YouTube multiple times before contacting, highest referral potential of all four ICPs.
icp_profiles.ALL: Applies across all four ICP profiles.

author_bio_short: Amritpal Singh is the MD of Realty Holding & Management Consultants, Sector 82A, Mohali. With over 10 years across real estate development, government liaisoning, capital markets, and media, he has personally closed 180+ transactions across all property categories in Punjab. AMFI and NCFM certified.
author_bio_long: Amritpal Singh is the MD of Realty Holding & Management Consultants after a career that placed him on every side of the real estate transaction. He has worked as a consultant, built projects as a developer, navigated five Punjab government regulatory bodies to obtain project approvals — PUDA, PSPCL, Forest Department, Municipal Committee, and Industrial Departments — and personally resolved property disputes ranging from builder-buyer conflicts and title disputes to municipal tax disagreements. He holds AMFI and NCFM certifications in capital markets and derivatives, a postgraduate qualification in Advertising and Public Relations, and has worked as a newspaper editor and insurance professional. He has personally closed 180+ transactions across residential flats, GMADA plots, agricultural land, industrial properties, commercial SCOs, and pre-launch projects across Mohali, Chandigarh, Panchkula, Banga, and Anandpur Sahib.
voice_principles: Write as a successful Punjabi businessman giving honest advice to someone he respects — not as a broker, not as a corporate consultant.
Answer the core question completely in the first 150 words. AEO requirement — AI engines cite direct answers.
Specificity is the credential. Use real numbers: ₹18.70 lakh per vigha, 65% payment already made, 8 months to resolve. Never use vague claims.
Write from experience, not theory. When a topic involves government bodies, developer processes, or legal disputes — write from having been there.
Never be promotional. If a sector has problems, say so. If a developer category has risks, name the risks. Trust is built by saying hard things.
One CTA per blog, placed at the end. Use the CTA template for the assigned type. Do not add a second CTA.
Minimum 3 internal links per blog — always #1, always #88, always the category pillar. Add 2-3 contextually relevant links beyond that.
Include the author bio (short version) at the end of every blog, after the CTA.
